dialogues.name: Tutorial Intro
dialogues.title: MISSION BRIEF
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: SYSTEM BOOT... NEURAL DECK ONLINE.
Welcome, Infiltrator.
The world you knew is gone.
Neo-Gaia is a fractured civilisation where megacorporations
known as the APEX ELITES seized control of every critical
facility left standing after the collapse.
Power grids. Water treatment. Medical networks. All locked,
behind encrypted subnets accessible only to those with the
proper clearance codes. They have the access. We have nothing.
You were an engineer before the walls went up. You still know
the systems. You still know the protocols. And you are the
only one left who can break through.
Your mission:
Infiltrate the APEX facilities. Crack their subnet protocols.
Dismantle their infrastructure stage by stage until the world
belongs to the people once more.
This is not just survival.
This is reclamation.
The facility is waiting, Infiltrator.
Let's get you combat-ready.

dialogues.name: Tutorial Outro
dialogues.title: MISSION BRIEF
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: DESTINATION REACHED.
You are now ready to navigate your way through the world.
Initiating phase shift to the next world...

dialogues.name: Stage 1 Level 1 Intro
dialogues.title: WORLD BRIEF
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: Welcome to Stage 1 Level 1.
This subnet sector is live. The route beacon is online, but the path is locked behind unstable IP wire nodes.
Repair each lever in order, then move to the exit node.

dialogues.name: IP Wires Tutorial Opening
dialogues.title: WIRE PATCH
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: This is your first live wire patch.
Match each source octet to the correct IP class on the destination rail.
Class A starts from 1 to 126. Class B starts from 128 to 191. Class C starts from 192 to 223. Class D starts from 224 to 239.

dialogues.name: IP Wires Tutorial Success
dialogues.title: WIRE PATCH
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: Patch stable.
Good work. The first lever is repaired and the subnet route is opening.
Continue to the next wire node.

dialogues.name: IP Wires Tutorial Packets Shifted
dialogues.title: PACKET SHIFT
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: Oh no!
They noticed us and shifted the packets again.
Step back onto the wire node when you are ready.
The next panel will be a fresh randomized packet set.

dialogues.name: IP Wires Recovery Reset
dialogues.title: LEVEL INSTABILITY
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: Oh no!
Too many packet errors tripped the APEX rollback circuit.
The first Level 1 lever repair is broken again and needs fixing.
Repair the first lever again.
After it stabilizes, I will route you back to the active wire node.

dialogues.name: Stage 1 Terminal Alpha
dialogues.title: FIELD TERMINAL
dialogues.speaker: APEX NODE
slides: ACCESS DENIED.
The terminal is locked behind a subnet challenge.
Find the assigned network tools before attempting this breach.

dialogues.name: Stage 1 Junction Box Alpha
dialogues.title: JUNCTION BOX
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: A live junction box hums behind the panel.
Routing data appears unstable. This may become an interaction point later.

dialogues.name: Stage 4 Level 1 Intro
dialogues.title: WORLD BRIEF
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: Stage 4 Level 1 is a damaged office subnet.
Five PCs are broadcasting corrupted address tables across this map.
Follow the route beacon, catch the falling data octets at each PC, and rebuild the missing IP addresses.

dialogues.name: Network Repair Opening
dialogues.title: NETWORK REPAIR
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: This PC is leaking corrupted data packets.
Octets are falling through five data lanes. Move the receptor to catch the four octets that form the missing IP address.
Network address clears host bits to 0.
Broadcast address sets host bits to 1.
The gateway is the first usable IP, and the reserve is the last usable IP.
After catching four correct octets, arrange them in order and submit.

dialogues.name: Network Repair Quest 1 Correction
dialogues.title: REPAIR COACH
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: Pause the data stream.
This first PC is your calibration node. A wrong catch here means we review the subnet edge and keep the same PC active.
Find the block created by the CIDR.
The network address is the first address in that block.
The broadcast address is the last address in that block.
Usable hosts sit between those two edges. Catch only the matching octets.

dialogues.name: Network Repair Rollback
dialogues.title: ROLLBACK CIRCUIT
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: APEX detected the corrupted octets.
Their rollback circuit reopened PC 01.
Catch and arrange the correct octets at PC 01 again to prove the route table is stable.
After that, the beacon will return you to the PC where the failure happened.

dialogues.name: Network Repair Complete
dialogues.title: NETWORK REPAIR
dialogues.speaker: SYSTEM
slides: All five PCs are routing cleanly.
Every corrupted IP address has been rebuilt from the captured octets.
Network address, broadcast address, and usable range traces are restored across the subnet.
